texto: I don't like talking unnecessarily, and my communication skills are zilch. I just can't converse with people. Maybe it's because of my stuttering or stammering, but I'm not confident of talking with people. I only talk to very close friends and family.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Pritam Chakraborty: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't like talking unnecessarily, and my communication skills are zilch. I just can't converse …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, family, friends pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e friends.

frase_original: I don't like talking unnecessarily, and my communication skills are zilch. I just can't converse with people. Maybe it's because of my stuttering or stammering, but I'm not confident of talking with people. I only talk to very close friends and family.
